keyboardInteraction.action: Moves focus to the next focusable element in the tab order.
Moves focus to the previous focusable element in the tab order.

keyboardInteraction.context: When focus is inside the dialog.

ariaAttribute.description: Applied automatically by the <button> element. Only set explicitly when using a non-button element.
Set to true when the button is non-interactive. Preferred over the HTML disabled attribute when the button should remain focusable for screen reader discoverability.
Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons that lack visible text.
Indicates whether a controlled element (menu, dialog, tooltip) is currently expanded.

colorContrast.context: Label text on primary button background in light mode.
Label text on danger button background in light mode.
Secondary button border and text against the default page background.

keyboardInteraction.action: Moves focus to the next focusable element.

ariaAttributes.description: Set to true when the button is non-interactive.
Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons.

accessibilityObject.screenReaderBehavior: Announced as '[label], button'. When disabled via aria-disabled, announced as '[label], button, dimmed' (VoiceOver) or '[label], button, unavailable' (NVDA/JAWS). When loading, announced as '[label], button, busy'.
accessibilityObject.focusManagement: The button participates in the normal tab order. It does not trap or redirect focus. When the button triggers a modal, focus is moved to the opened element by the modal component, not the button.

colorContrast.context: Label text on primary button background in light mode.

accessibilityObject.motionConsiderations: The loading spinner animation respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query. When reduced motion is preferred, the spinner is replaced with a static ellipsis indicator.
